summary: The shared renderer and tests are mostly in place, and `npm run test:unit` plus `npm run verify` both pass, but the implementation is not safe enough to promote yet. In [src/features/notes/markdown.ts](/Users/stevenna/WebstormProjects/minakeep/src/features/notes/markdown.ts:109), `extractReferenceDefinitions()` removes every syntactically supported `[^label]: [Title](url)` definition from the article before the renderer knows whether that label is ever referenced. Later, only actually-used labels are emitted into `references` ([src/features/notes/markdown.ts](/Users/stevenna/WebstormProjects/minakeep/src/features/notes/markdown.ts:184)). That means an authored supported definition with no matching inline `[^label]` marker silently disappears from both the article body and the bottom `References` section. This violates the task’s requirement for a narrow, explicit, safe contract and leaves unmatched-but-supported syntax with destructive behavior rather than safe fallback.
satisfied_exit_criteria: The renderer now emits a reusable `articleHtml + references` contract and the owner preview/public note surfaces consume it.
Supported referenced labels are deduplicated into one stable rendered entry in first-use order.
missing_requirements: Safe fallback behavior is incomplete: supported-looking reference definitions without a matching inline marker are dropped entirely instead of remaining visible in the article body or being rendered in `References`.
Unit coverage does not protect that fallback case, so the current contract still allows silent content loss for authored markdown.
human_review_triggers: Silent content loss for orphaned supported reference definitions in `src/features/notes/markdown.ts`.
Promotion criteria explicitly call for a narrow, explicit, safe renderer contract; this case remains undefined/destructive.
